ucs.title: [LLM] APT37 (ScarCruft) ROKRAT C2 over legitimate cloud storage APIs (pCloud / Yandex / Dropbox)
ucs.description: Hunts ROKRAT / Chinotto / GoldBackdoor beaconing where the implant abuses pCloud, Yandex Disk, or Dropbox APIs for bidirectional C2 from a host process that just executed a weaponized HWP/Office/LNK lure. This cloud-API-as-C2 pattern is a signature of ScarCruft / Reaper / InkySquid since at least 2018.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Web.url) as url values(Web.dest) as dest values(Web.user_agent) as ua from datamodel=Web where (Web.url="*api.pcloud.com*" OR Web.url="*cloud-api.yandex.net*" OR Web.url="*content.dropboxapi.com*" OR Web.url="*api.dropboxapi.com*" OR Web.url="*api-content.dropbox.com*") by host Web.src Web.process_name Web.app
| `drop_dm_object_name(Web)`
| search process_name!="chrome.exe" process_name!="msedge.exe" process_name!="firefox.exe" process_name!="dropbox.exe" process_name!="onedrive.exe" process_name!="yandexdisk.exe" process_name!="pcloud.exe" process_name!="iexplore.exe" process_name!="opera.exe" process_name!="brave.exe"
| join type=inner host [
    | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("hwp.exe","hwp32.exe","hwp64.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","explorer.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe")) by host Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process Processes.user _time
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
    | rename _time as exec_time
    | where exec_time > relative_time(now(),"-7d")
]
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
| table host src process_name parent_process_name url ua firstTime lastTime count
ucs.kql:
let lure_exec = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("hwp.exe","hwp32.exe","hwp64.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","explorer.exe")
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe")
| project DeviceId, DeviceName, ExecTime=Timestamp, Loader=FileName, Parent=InitiatingProcessFileName, Cmd=ProcessCommandLine;
let cloud_c2 = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where RemoteUrl has_any ("api.pcloud.com","cloud-api.yandex.net","content.dropboxapi.com","api.dropboxapi.com","api-content.dropbox.com")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","iexplore.exe","brave.exe","opera.exe","dropbox.exe","onedrive.exe","yandexdisk.exe","pcloud.exe")
| project DeviceId, DeviceName, NetTime=Timestamp, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, BeaconProc=InitiatingProcessFileName, BeaconCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
lure_exec
| join kind=inner cloud_c2 on DeviceId
| where (NetTime - ExecTime) between (0min .. 60min)
| summarize Hits=count(), FirstBeacon=min(NetTime), LastBeacon=max(NetTime), URLs=make_set(RemoteUrl,25), IPs=make_set(RemoteIP,25)
    by DeviceName, Parent, Loader, BeaconProc, Cmd
| order by Hits desc
ucs.rationale: ROKRAT (and successors Chinotto / GoldBackdoor) are essentially exclusive to APT37/ScarCruft and the operators have consistently chosen pCloud, Yandex Disk, and Dropbox REST endpoints for both task retrieval and exfil — a pattern documented by Cisco Talos, Volexity, Kaspersky and ESET across 2018-2023. Pairing the cloud-API beacon with a recent HWP/Office/LNK-spawned LOLBAS loader on the same host

ucs.title: [LLM] APT37 weaponized HWP/LNK lure → in-memory PowerShell shellcode loader (ROKRAT/GoldBackdoor stager)
ucs.description: Detects APT37's characteristic delivery chain targeting Korean-speaking victims (defectors, journalists, ROK think-tanks): a Hangul (HWP) document or oversized LNK launches a shell that base64/hex-decodes and reflectively loads ROKRAT/Chinotto shellcode, often via DLL side-loading of a legitimate signed binary.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmd values(Processes.process_id) as pid values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("hwp.exe","hwp32.exe","hwp64.exe","explorer.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe")) by host Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| eval susp_b64=if(match(cmd,"(?i)(FromBase64String|::Decompress|GzipStream|DeflateStream)"),1,0)
| eval susp_inj=if(match(cmd,"(?i)(VirtualAlloc|WriteProcessMemory|CreateRemoteThread|Reflection\.Assembly|Invoke-ReflectivePEInjection|Add-Type)"),1,0)
| eval susp_lnk=if(match(cmd,"(?i)(\.lnk|\.hwp|forfiles|findstr|certutil)"),1,0)
| eval score=susp_b64+susp_inj+susp_lnk
| where score>=2
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
| table host parent_process_name process_name user pid cmd score firstTime lastTime
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("hwp.exe","hwp32.exe","hwp64.exe","explorer.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe")
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe")
| extend Cmd = tolower(ProcessCommandLine)
| extend SuspB64   = iff(Cmd matches regex @"(frombase64string|::decompress|gzipstream|deflatestream|-enc(odedcommand)?\s)", 1, 0)
| extend SuspInj   = iff(Cmd matches regex @"(virtualalloc|writeprocessmemory|createremotethread|reflection\.assembly|invoke-reflectivepeinjection|add-type|memorystream)", 1, 0)
| extend SuspLure  = iff(Cmd matches regex @"(\.lnk|\.hwp|forfiles|findstr |certutil |bitsadmin )", 1, 0)
| extend SuspKR    = iff(InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any (".hwp","한글","통일","조선","북한") or Cmd has_any (".hwp","\\appdata\\roaming\\hnc\\"), 1, 0)
| extend Score = SuspB64 + SuspInj + SuspLure + SuspKR
| where Score >= 2
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceImageLoadEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"\appdata\", @"\programdata\", @"\users\public\")
    | where InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has_any ("Microsoft","Adobe","Hancom","Kakao","Naver")
    | project DeviceId, SideloadedDll=FileName, SideloadHost=InitiatingProcessFileName, SideloadTime=Timestamp
) on DeviceId
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, Score, SideloadedDll, SideloadHost, SideloadTime
| order by Score desc, Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: APT37 is one of the very few clusters that routinely weaponizes Hangul Word Processor (HWP) documents — Hancom is dominant only in Korean victim environments, so an `hwp.exe` parent spawning powershell/mshta is already a strong actor signal. Layering decode+inject indicators (their ROKRAT/GoldBackdoor shellcode loader pattern, documented by Stairwell, Volexity 'BLUELIGHT' / 'Dolphin', and ESET) pl
